close_reason: Split env::var error into NotPresent and NotUnicode messages
description: src/lib.rs:126-129 uses .map_err(|_| ...) on std::env::var, discarding the std::env::VarError. If BWS_ACCESS_TOKEN is set but not valid UTF-8 (e.g. someone sourced a tainted shell profile), the user sees 'BWS_ACCESS_TOKEN environment variable is not set' which is wrong. Match on the VarError variants: NotPresent → 'not set', NotUnicode(_) → 'set but contains non-UTF-8 bytes'. Cheap fix, improves debuggability when something weird is happening.
title: env::var error discriminator collapsed: NotPresent and NotUnicode share one message
